behaviors.description: Storing a PASS review summary is blocked when any stored dimension finding for the same round has a FAIL verdict.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at module handoff (summary write path must read persisted per-dimension findings for that round). No real OS/network/subprocess needed; no AI output-quality dependency. Rejected higher levels because behavior text is purely about "stored ... finding" consistency checks, not live agent execution.
behaviors.test_description: Low (Unit): call validator(findings, summary=PASS); assert reject; catches boolean logic bug; misses repo query bug or wrong round scoping. Current (Integration): seed findings table for round R with one FAIL, call summary-store service with PASS for R, assert write blocked and reason references failing dimension; catches cross-module query/filter mismatch; misses real API/process faults. High (System): exercise via full service/API + real DB process; catches deployment wiring issues; misses little beyond integration for this behavior.

behaviors.description: Stop-gate verdict computation reads per-dimension stored findings directly and does not rely only on hand-written summary text.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is gate layer choosing wrong data source when wired to storage. Needs multiple modules (gate + findings store + summary store) but not real infra or model output. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text targets data-source selection, not model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Low (Unit): gateVerdict(summaryText, findings) with injected objects; catches local precedence bug; misses persistence lookup mistakes. Current (Integration): persist contradictory state (summary says PASS, stored finding FAIL), run stop-gate service, assert FAIL/escalate based on stored findings; catches real regression from trusting text. High (System): through full runtime boundary; catches process/config issues but not required for stated failure.

behaviors.description: Manual summary text overrides require an explicit override flag and produce an auditable warning record.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans request handling, authorization/flag check, and audit persistence. Unit is too low if audit write or flag propagation fails across modules. No mandatory real OS/network/model dependency in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Low (Unit): function validateOverride(flag,text) rejects missing flag; catches pure logic bug; misses missing audit-write side effect. Current (Integration): call summary-save path with manual override text once without flag (assert reject), once with flag (assert allowed) and verify warning row/event persisted with actor+round; catches missing side-effect wiring. High (System): include external log sink; catches sink connectivity, which is beyond stated failure mode.

behaviors.description: After Phase 4 fixes, the gate requires a review round identifier produced after the latest fix commit before PASS can be finalized.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is stale-state correlation across commit metadata and review-round metadata. Needs orchestration across commit-tracking and gate modules. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because text requires temporal linkage ("produced after latest fix commit"), not model output correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Low (Unit): compareRoundTimestampVsFixCommit() with fixtures; catches ordering logic bug; misses wrong data fetch from stores. Current (Integration): seed fix commit C2 and review rounds where latest is tied to C1 only; run finalize gate, assert PASS blocked until round linked to >=C2 exists; catches stale-round acceptance bug. High (System): invoke real VCS/subprocess to produce commits and full app path; catches git integration bugs but not strictly required by behavior wording.

behaviors.description: In the live review loop, PASS cannot finalize unless a fresh review-agent round runs on the updated diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a real review-agent round in live loop. A stubbed/fake agent run can hide failures where orchestration marks a run fresh without actually executing the agent on updated diff. Single agentic decision boundary; not necessarily multi-agent sequence, so Agentic over Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Low (Integration): fake agent-run record inserted post-fix, assert gate checks recency; catches metadata checks; misses failures where live runner skips/short-circuits real agent execution. Current (Agentic): apply fix commit, execute real review-agent round on updated diff, then attempt finalize; assert PASS blocked before run and allowed only after successful fresh run id; catches no-rerun regression in live loop. High (Workflow): multi-stage pipeline with planner/critic/tooling; catches broader pipeline coupling issues, but exceeds this single-round freshness requirement.

behaviors.description: If review rounds are exhausted with unresolved MUST-FIX findings in stored results, the outcome is escalation rather than PASS.
behaviors.justification: Decision uses stored findings + round counters; failure appears when policy engine and persistence interact. No requirement for real live agent call in this behavior text (stored results already present). Rejected Agentic because correctness here is policy evaluation over persisted verdicts, not model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Low (Unit): evaluateOutcome(roundsUsed,maxRounds,findings) => escalation; catches policy-branch bug; misses persistence/query mapping errors. Current (Integration): seed stored MUST-FIX unresolved findings and roundsUsed=max; run outcome computation path; assert escalation state and PASS blocked; catches real state-loading regressions. High (Workflow): run full live rounds until exhausted; catches upstream agent-run issues but not required for this stored-results behavior.

behaviors.description: In a live multi-round review workflow, exhausted rounds with unresolved MUST-FIX findings still block PASS and emit escalation.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets live multi-round pipeline with sequential review rounds and terminal escalation. Multiple agentic steps are part of the stated failure boundary; single-step Agentic is too low. Rejected lower levels because they can simulate final state but miss sequencing bugs across rounds/handoffs in live workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Low (Integration): pre-seed final exhausted state and assert escalation; catches final decision bug; misses transition/sequence defects across rounds. Current (Workflow): execute real multi-round loop (round1..max) with unresolved MUST-FIX persisting, then finalize; assert PASS blocked and escalation event emitted; catches cross-round state carryover and terminal-gate sequencing bugs. High (System): add external infra chaos/network faults; catches operational resilience issues beyond stated behavior.
